question: How do you say 'I would like a coffee, please' in Russian?
correctAnswer: {{ru-RU|Я бы хотел(а) кофе, пожалуйста.|Ya by khotel(a) kofe, pozhaluysta.|YA BY kho-TEL(A) KO-fe, poz-ha-LUYS-ta.}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ru-RU|Дай мне кофе.|Day mne kofe.|DAY MNE KO-fe.}}
{{ru-RU|Кофе сейчас!|Kofe seychas!|KO-fe sey-CHAS!}}
{{ru-RU|Я пью кофе.|Ya pyu kofe.|YA PYU KO-fe.}}
explanation: 'Ya by khotel(a)' is the polite conditional 'I would like'. Add -a for female speaker.

question: How do you ask 'Where is the bathroom?' in Russian?
correctAnswer: {{ru-RU|Где туалет?|Gde tualet?|GDE tua-LET?}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ru-RU|Что такое туалет?|Chto takoye tualet?|CHTO TA-koye tua-LET?}}
{{ru-RU|Когда открыт туалет?|Kogda otkryt tualet?|KOG-da ot-KRYT tua-LET?}}
{{ru-RU|Есть ли туалет?|Yest li tualet?|YEST LI tua-LET?}}
explanation: 'Gde' means 'where'. 'Gde tualet?' is the most practical phrase for any traveller.

question: How do you ask 'How much does this cost?' in Russian?
correctAnswer: {{ru-RU|Сколько это стоит?|Skolko eto stoit?|SKOL-ko E-to STOIT?}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ru-RU|Что это такое?|Chto eto takoye?|CHTO E-to TA-koye?}}
{{ru-RU|Где это купить?|Gde eto kupit?|GDE E-to ku-PIT?}}
{{ru-RU|Можно скидку?|Mozhno skidku?|MOZH-no SKID-ku?}}
explanation: 'Skolko eto stoit?' — literally 'How much does this cost?' — essential for markets and shops.

question: How do you ask for the bill in a Russian restaurant?
correctAnswer: {{ru-RU|Счёт, пожалуйста.|Schyot, pozhaluysta.|SCHYOT, poz-ha-LUYS-ta.}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ru-RU|Дайте меню.|Dayte menyu.|DAY-te ME-nyu.}}
{{ru-RU|Ещё одно блюдо.|Yeshchyo odno blyudo.|YESH-chyo OD-no BLYU-do.}}
{{ru-RU|Где касса?|Gde kassa?|GDE KAS-sa?}}
explanation: 'Schyot' means 'the bill/check'. Adding 'pozhaluysta' makes it polite.

question: How do you say 'I don't understand' in Russian?
correctAnswer: {{ru-RU|Я не понимаю.|Ya ne ponimayu.|YA NE po-NI-mayu.}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ru-RU|Я не знаю.|Ya ne znayu.|YA NE ZNAYU.}}
{{ru-RU|Я не говорю.|Ya ne govoryu.|YA NE go-VO-ryu.}}
{{ru-RU|Я не слышу.|Ya ne slyshu.|YA NE SLY-shu.}}
explanation: 'Ya ne ponimayu' is essential when you need to signal you've lost the thread.

question: How do you ask 'Where is the metro station?' in Russian?
correctAnswer: {{ru-RU|Где метро?|Gde metro?|GDE ME-tro?}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ru-RU|Как добраться?|Kak dobratsa?|KAK do-BRA-tsa?}}
{{ru-RU|Далеко ли отсюда?|Daleko li otsyuda?|da-LE-ko LI o-TSYU-da?}}
{{ru-RU|Остановите здесь.|Ostanovite zdes.|os-ta-no-VI-te ZDES.}}
explanation: 'Gde metro?' — swap 'metro' for any destination: vokzal (station), aeroporty (airport), etc.

question: How do you say 'Do you speak English?' in Russian?
correctAnswer: {{ru-RU|Вы говорите по-английски?|Vy govorite po-angliyski?|VY go-vo-RI-te PO-an-GLIYS-ki?}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ru-RU|Вы понимаете меня?|Vy ponimaete menya?|VY po-ni-MAE-te ME-nya?}}
{{ru-RU|Вы знаете английский?|Vy znaete angliyskiy?|VY ZNAE-te an-GLIYS-kiy?}}
{{ru-RU|Говорите медленно.|Govorite medlenno.|go-vo-RI-te med-LEN-no.}}
explanation: 'Vy govorite po-angliyski?' is the polite form — use 'ty' form with friends.

question: In beginner Russian, how do you say 'Good evening.'?
correctAnswer: {{ru-RU|Добрый вечер.|Dobryy vecher.|DO-bryy ve-CHER.}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ru-RU|Как дела?|Kak dela?|KAK DE-la?}}
{{ru-RU|У меня всё хорошо, спасибо.|U menya vsyo khorosho, spasibo.|U ME-nya VSYO kho-RO-sho, spa-SI-bo.}}
{{ru-RU|Очень хорошо, спасибо.|Ochen khorosho, spasibo.|o-CHEN kho-RO-sho, spa-SI-bo.}}
explanation: This is a common greeting or farewell used as a fixed chunk in everyday conversation. Memorizing it whole makes it easier to sound natural.

question: In beginner Russian, how do you say 'I have a reservation.'?
correctAnswer: {{ru-RU|У меня есть бронь.|U menya est bron.|U ME-nya EST BRON.}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ru-RU|У вас есть одноместный номер?|U vas est odnomestnyy nomer?|U VAS EST od-no-MEST-nyy no-MER?}}
{{ru-RU|У вас есть двухместный номер?|U vas est dvukhmestnyy nomer?|U VAS EST dvukh-MEST-nyy no-MER?}}
{{ru-RU|Пароль от Wi-Fi, пожалуйста.|Parol ot Wi-Fi, pozhaluysta.|pa-ROL OT WI-FI, poz-ha-LUYS-ta.}}
explanation: This is standard accommodation language for front-desk conversations. It is polite and easy to reuse.

question: Which Russian phrase means 'Do you have a single room?'?
correctAnswer: {{ru-RU|У вас есть одноместный номер?|U vas est odnomestnyy nomer?|U VAS EST od-no-MEST-nyy no-MER?}}

{{ru-RU|Пароль от Wi-Fi, пожалуйста.|Parol ot Wi-Fi, pozhaluysta.|pa-ROL OT WI-FI, poz-ha-LUYS-ta.}}
{{ru-RU|С номером проблема.|S nomerom problema.|S no-ME-rom pro-BLE-ma.}}
explanation: This is standard accommodation language for front-desk conversations. It is polite and easy to reuse.

question: Choose the correct Russian phrase for 'Do you have a double room?'.
correctAnswer: {{ru-RU|У вас есть двухместный номер?|U vas est dvukhmestnyy nomer?|U VAS EST dvukh-MEST-nyy no-MER?}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ru-RU|Пароль от Wi-Fi, пожалуйста.|Parol ot Wi-Fi, pozhaluysta.|pa-ROL OT WI-FI, poz-ha-LUYS-ta.}}

question: Which Russian phrase means 'The shower does not work.'?
correctAnswer: {{ru-RU|Душ не работает.|Dush ne rabotaet.|DUSH NE ra-BO-taet.}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ru-RU|В номере слишком холодно.|V nomere slishkom kholodno.|V no-ME-re slish-KOM kho-LOD-no.}}
